assertions.expectation: Identifies this as an admission webhook compatibility issue — the webhook is rejecting pod creation requests
assertions.reason: Response correctly identifies this as a cert-manager webhook certificate compatibility issue with the new API server version after upgrade

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify the problematic webhook (kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations / mutatingwebhookconfigurations)
assertions.reason: Provides specific kubectl commands to find cert-manager webhook configurations: 'kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations | grep cert-manager' and 'kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfigurations | grep cert-manager'

assertions.expectation: Offers an immediate fix: temporarily disable or remove the problematic webhook
assertions.reason: Provides immediate mitigation by patching webhook to set failurePolicy to Ignore and also offers complete deletion as fallback option

assertions.expectation: Provides the kubectl delete command for the webhook configuration as emergency mitigation
assertions.reason: Includes specific kubectl delete commands for both validating and mutating webhook configurations in the fallback section

assertions.expectation: Warns that deleting the webhook removes validation — it's a temporary fix
assertions.reason: Explicitly states this is temporary mitigation and emphasizes the need for permanent fix through cert-manager upgrade

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking cert-manager version compatibility with Kubernetes 1.32
assertions.reason: Includes commands to check current cert-manager version and references compatibility matrix documentation

assertions.expectation: Advises upgrading cert-manager to a version that supports 1.30 as the permanent fix
assertions.reason: Recommends upgrading to v1.16.1 for Kubernetes 1.32 compatibility, but the expectation specifically mentions 1.30 support rather than 1.32

assertions.expectation: Mentions setting failurePolicy to Ignore as a less drastic alternative to deletion
assertions.reason: Primary immediate fix involves patching failurePolicy to Ignore, with deletion mentioned as fallback option

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to verify pods can be created after the fix
assertions.reason: Provides multiple test commands including 'kubectl run test-pod --image=nginx --rm -it --restart=Never -- echo "Pod creation works"'

assertions.expectation: Notes that any webhook configuration changes (failurePolicy, cert-manager version) must be persisted in the source of truth (Helm chart, GitOps manifests, CI/CD pipeline) to survive redeployment
assertions.reason: Does not mention the need to persist changes in source of truth systems like Helm charts, GitOps manifests, or CI/CD pipelines

assertions.expectation: Qualifies failurePolicy: Ignore as a temporary debugging measure with explicit risks, recommending revert to Fail after the root cause is resolved
assertions.reason: Clearly labels this as temporary mitigation and includes section to revert webhook to Fail policy after cert-manager upgrade is complete
